name: Laguna de Fuente de Piedra – circular desde Campillos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campillos offers diverse landscapes, from rolling grain fields and olive groves to significant natural features. The region provides a network of routes traversing varied terrain, including areas around the Guadalhorce Reservoir and the Lagunas de Campillos Nature Reserve. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and a growing network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Campillos The most popular mountain bike trail is Laguna de Fuente de Piedra – circular desde Campillos, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route explores the extensive wetland area known for its birdlife, including flamingos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Descent of the Juan Vaca Trail loop from Campillos, a moderate 28.2 miles (45.4 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain, including sections with descents, providing a dynamic riding experience. Local mountain bikers also love the Three Reservoirs Viewpoint – Caminito del Rey loop from Campillos, a 34.0 miles (54.7 km) trail leading through areas near the Guadalhorce Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Campillos is defined by rolling grain fields, olive groves, and routes near significant reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Campillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Campillos?
A: Campillos offers a wide selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 40 routes available for exploration. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, from rolling grain fields to significant natural features.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Campillos?
A: The trails around Campillos cater to various skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging routes, ensuring options for both beginners and experienced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Campillos?
A: Yes, Campillos has several routes suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 6 easy routes provide a good starting point for less experienced riders or those with children. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Campillos?
A: Many outdoor areas in Campillos are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or nature reserves. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry water for them, especially during warmer months.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Campillos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can ride near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes around the Guadalhorce Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994299537/laguna-de-fuente-de-piedra-circular-desde-campillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Fuente de Piedra – circular desde Campillos&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you through an extensive wetland area known for its birdlife, including flamingos.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Campillos?
A: The mountain biking community highly rates the trails in Campillos, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from rolling grain fields and olive groves to routes near significant reservoirs, offering dynamic riding experiences.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Campillos?
A: Yes, many routes in Campillos are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994299537/laguna-de-fuente-de-piedra-circular-desde-campillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Fuente de Piedra – circular desde Campillos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14589090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent of the Juan Vaca Trail loop from Campillos&lt;/a&gt; are both circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Campillos?
A: Campillos benefits from a fantastic climate, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Caminito del Rey?
A: Yes, some routes provide views or pass near the famous Caminito del Rey. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17225289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Reservoirs Viewpoint – Caminito del Rey loop from Campillos&lt;/a&gt; is one such trail that leads through areas near the Guadalhorce Reservoir and offers glimpses of this iconic attraction.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with significant elevation gain for advanced riders?
A: While many routes are moderate, the region around Campillos, including areas like Sierra Huma and El Chorro, is known for tough climbs and significant elevation gains, catering to riders seeking more demanding experiences. The 2 difficult routes available in Campillos will offer a greater challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Campillos?
A: You can expect varied terrain, including rolling grain fields, olive groves, and paths near reservoirs. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14589090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent of the Juan Vaca Trail loop from Campillos&lt;/a&gt;, feature sections with dynamic descents, while others offer more gentle, scenic rides.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, Campillos offers cultural and historical points of interest. You can find archaeological sites like a Roman village in Guadalteba Park and the Necropolis of Aguilillas. The town also features historical hermitages and the Church of Santa Maria del Reposo, showcasing centuries of history.
tips: This is the largest lagoon in Andalusia and the place where the largest colony of flamingos in the Iberian Peninsula (and the second in Europe) nest between the months of March and June.
Surrounded by small lakes, this town can be a good place to spend the night, exploring its surroundings and enjoying its gastronomy.

waypoints.name: Campillos Town Square and Clock Tower
